# GTM Strategy: Auto-Generated One-Page Preview Sites for Businesses Without Websites

---

## 1. TARGETING

### Ideal Customer Profile (ICP)

**Primary:** Local micro-businesses and sole proprietors who have:
- A physical location or service area
- Existing customers / foot traffic / word-of-mouth
- No current web presence (or a dated, broken site)
- Limited time/technical skills to build their own
- A business name, basic info, and at least one way to be contacted

**Verticals to Prioritize:**
| Vertical | Why | "Foot in the Door" |
|---|---|---|
| Restaurants / Cafés | High need, photo-driven, menu = easy content | "See how your menu could look online" |
| Home services (plumbers, electricians, landscapers) | High search intent, no website common | "Get found when people Google [service] in [city]" |
| Salons, barbers, aestheticians | Appointment-driven, Instagram-heavy | "Turn your Instagram into a booking page" |
| Real estate agents | Always hungry for leads, mobile-first | "A page that generates leads, not just vibes" |
| Wedding vendors (photographers, DJs, florists) | Portfolio-heavy, seasonal urgency | "Show couples what you do before they meet you" |
| Coaches, consultants, tutors | Simple content, trust-driven | "A page that sells while you sleep" |

**Secondary:** Niche retail shops, fitness instructors, pet services, event spaces, community organizations.

### Data Sources for Prospecting
- Google Maps / Business Listings (scrape or API)
- Yelp, TripAdvisor, Houzz, WeddingWire (no-site indicators)
- Local chamber of commerce directories
- Instagram business accounts (no link in bio → linktree)
- Cold outreach lists via Clearbit, Apollo, LinkedIn Sales Navigator

---

## 2. MESSAGING

### Core Value Proposition

> **"We made a free preview of what your business could look like online — claim it, edit it, or we'll build you the real thing."**

Three angles depending on audience:

| Angle | Headline | Use Case |
|---|---|---|
| **Vanity** | "Your business deserves to look this good online" | Social media, visual-heavy businesses |
| **Utility** | "Get found. Get called. Get paid." | Service businesses, home pros |
| **Risk-reversal** | "No contract. No credit card. Just a better first impression." | Skeptical SMB owners |

### Positioning Statement

> **[Product]** creates beautiful, mobile-ready one-page websites for businesses that don't have one — automatically, using publicly available business data — and lets business owners claim, customize, and upgrade to a full site whenever they're ready.**

### Key Messages by Stage

**Awareness:** "Your competitors are getting found online. Are you?"
**Consideration:** "We built a preview of your website — for free. Here's what it looks like."
**Conversion:** "Your page is live. Now claim it, change it, or let us build you a full site."

---

## 3. OUTREACH CHANNELS

### Channel Strategy

| Channel | Tactic | Why |
|---|---|---|
| **Cold Email** | Personalized 1:1 emails with preview link embedded | Highest volume, scalable |
| **Cold DM (Instagram/Facebook)** | Slide into DMs with preview screenshot + short hook | Visual businesses, high reply rates |
| **Google Business Message** | Message businesses directly via their GBP listing | Low competition, warm intent |
| **Yelp/WeddingWire inquiry** | Pose as a potential customer, then offer | Reveals pain, builds rapport |
| **LinkedIn** | For B2B-adjacent businesses (consultants, coaches, B2B services) | Professional context |
| **SMS** (with consent) | Via business texting tools, follow-up to email | High open rate |
| **QR codes** | Place in physical locations (partner with POS providers) | Offline-to-online bridge |
| **Franchise/association partnerships** | White-label or referral through industry groups | Warm referral, volume |

### Outreach Sequence (Multi-touch)

```
Day 0:  Email #1 — The preview reveal + "what do you think?" hook
Day 3:  SMS/Email #2 — "Did you get a chance to look at it?"
Day 7:  DM #1 — Visual message with screenshot
Day 14: Email #3 — Case study from similar business + soft CTA
Day 21: Final email — "We're retiring free previews on [date]" (artificial urgency)
```

---

## 4. OFFER STRUCTURE

### The Preview (Lead Magnet / Value Demo)

- **What it is:** A fully functional, auto-generated one-page website built from public data (name, address, photos, reviews, social)
- **What's included:** Business info, photo(s), hours, map, contact form, basic services
- **Time to generate:** Minutes, fully automated
- **Cost to prospect:** Free
- **Upsell path:** Full site, custom domain, SEO, booking widget, marketing tools

### Upgrade Tiers

| Tier | Name | Price (Monthly) | What's Included |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Free** | Preview | $0 | One-page site, `[brand].preview.com`, 1 revision |
| **Starter** | Go Live | $29/mo | Custom domain, no branded footer, Google Analytics, contact form |
| **Growth** | Get Found | $79/mo | Starter + SEO basics, Google Business sync, social links, removal of preview branding |
| **Pro** | Get Booked | $149/mo | Growth + appointment/booking widget, email capture, priority support |
| **Agency** | White Label | $499/mo | Unlimited client sites, white-label, API access, reselling rights |

**Annual discount:** 2 months free (effectively ~17% off)

### Conversion Mechanics
- Free preview = no friction entry
- "Claim your page" requires email only (lowest barrier)
- Upgrade CTA appears contextually (on the page itself, in dashboard)
- Nudge: "Your preview expires in X days" (even if it doesn't — behavioral urgency)
- Referral: "Refer a business, get 1 month free"

---

```
FREE                    STARTER ($29/mo)          GROWTH ($79/mo)           PRO ($149/mo)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
One-page site           Custom domain              All of Starter +          All of Growth +
preview domain          No preview footer          Google Business sync      Booking widget
1 revision              Analytics                  SEO basics                Email capture
Contact form            Social links               Social integrations      Priority support
Map + hours             Email support                                        

[Claim Free]            [Go Live - $29/mo]        [Get Found - $79/mo]      [Get Booked - $149/mo]
```

---

## 6. OBJECTION HANDLING

### "I don't need a website"

**Response:** "Totally fair. Most of our customers said the same thing — right until a customer asked for their URL and they had nothing to show. This is less about needing a website and more about not losing the next person who Google-searches you."

### "I already have a Facebook/Instagram page"

**Response:** "That's great! But Facebook buries your business info behind an app. A one-page site is like your digital storefront — it's what shows up in Google, it's what you can text to a customer in 2 seconds, and it doesn't disappear if the algorithm changes."

### "I don't have time to manage it"

**Response:** "You don't have to. Your preview is already built. You can claim it in 5 minutes, or let us know what to change and we'll do it. And once it's live, it works 24/7 without you touching it."

### "It's too expensive" / "I'm not sure the ROI is there"

**Response:** "What would one new customer per month be worth to you? For most of our [vertical] customers, that's a pays-for-itself moment right there. And you're not locked in — month-to-month, no contracts."

### "Who else is using this?" / "Show me social proof"

**Response:** "We've generated previews for [X] businesses like yours. [Show 2-3 similar vertical examples.] Here's what [Business Name] said after upgrading: '[Testimonial]'"

### "I need to think about it"

**Response:** "No rush. Your preview is live for the next [X] days. When you're ready, claim it and it's yours. If you'd like a quick 10-minute walkthrough to see how it works, I'm happy to set that up."

### "Can you send me more information?"

**Response:** "Absolutely — [landing page URL]. But honestly the preview IS the information. It's literally what your business would look like online. Want me to send you the link right now?"

### "I already paid someone to build me a site"

**Response:** "Congrats! How's it working for you? [Listen.] A lot of business owners we talk to have sites that are either outdated, broken on mobile, or they can't edit themselves. If that's not hitting the mark, we'd love to show you the comparison."

---

## 7. LEGAL & ETHICAL CONSIDERATIONS

### Data Privacy & Consent

**The Core Issue:** You're generating and displaying a website about a business owner without their explicit prior consent. This is the central legal/ethical risk.

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Generating a site using scraped public data (address, hours, reviews) | Ensure all data sources are publicly available (Google Maps, Yelp public listings, social media). Do NOT scrape private data. |
| Displaying business info without permission | Include prominent "Claim Your Page" / "Opt-Out" mechanism on every preview. Make removal frictionless. |
| Sending unsolicited email/DM with the preview link | Use opt-out language. In email: pre-checked unsubscribe link. In DM: immediate opt-out instruction. Comply with CAN-SPAM, GDPR (EU prospects), CASL (Canada). |
| Including real customer reviews | Reviews are third-party content — attribute source. Don't imply the reviews are yours orfabricated. |
| Implying an existing business relationship | Clearly state: "We generated this free preview for you — no obligation." |

### Legal Checklist

- [ ] **Opt-out mechanism** on every preview page ("Remove this page" / "Claim & Manage")
- [ ] **Privacy policy** posted on main marketing site
- [ ] **Unsubscribe link** in every cold email (CAN-SPAM requirement)
- [ ] **Data sources documented** — what we scraped, where from, how to request deletion
- [ ] **No misleading subject lines** — don't imply existing relationship in email subject
- [ ] **GDPR:** If targeting EU businesses, add consent capture or legitimate interest justification
- [ ] **No phone number / SMS without prior express consent** (TCPA for US)
- [ ] **No impersonation** — don't pose as a customer in DMs if it could constitute impersonation
- [ ] **Transparent about automated generation** — don't let them think a human built it specially for them without clarifying it's auto-generated

### Ethical Guardrails

- **Don't create fake reviews or fake testimonials.** Ever.
- **Don't imply endorsement** (e.g., "As seen in..." without documentation)
- **Don't use dark patterns** on the upgrade/claim flow (buried cancel, pre-checked upgrades)
- **Honor opt-outs immediately and permanently** — one unsubscribe = no re-contact
- **Be honest about what's auto-generated vs. human-curated** — builds trust, reduces churn
- **Never threaten** (e.g., "your page will expire and be deleted" if that isn't actually true)

### Recommended Legal Language for Outreach

Add to bottom of every cold email:
> "You're receiving this because we generated a free preview website for [Business Name] using publicly available information. This is not a sales call — we're simply showing you what your business could look like online. To claim, edit, or remove your preview, click here: [manage link]. To unsubscribe from future emails, click here: [unsubscribe]."

---

## 8. EMAIL TEMPLATE

### Cold Email — Initial Outreach (The Preview Reveal)

**Subject:** We made something for [Business Name] 🎨

---

Hi [First Name],

I came across [Business Name] while looking at [city] businesses and we automatically built a preview of what your website could look like.

Here's the link: [preview-link]

It includes your business info, location, hours, and a way for customers to contact you — all on one page that looks great on phones.

No catch. It's free to claim, free to edit, and you can upgrade to a full custom site whenever you want. Or don't — the preview stays up either way.

Curious what you think. Happy to make any changes or walk you through how it works.

Cheers,
[Your Name]
[Your Company]
[manage/unsubscribe link]

---

### Follow-Up Email #1 (Day 3)

**Subject:** Re: We made something for [Business Name] 🎨

---

Hi [First Name],

Just following up on the preview I sent over — wanted to make sure it landed.

Here's the link again: [preview-link]

To save you a click: it took us about 2 minutes to build, it loads in under 1 second on mobile, and [mention one specific thing we included — e.g., "I pulled your hours from Google so they're already right"].

Worth a 30-second look, I promise.

—
[Your Name]
[unsubscribe link]

---

### Follow-Up Email #2 (Day 14 — Social Proof Angle)

**Subject:** What a [business type] in [city] said about their preview

---

Hi [First Name],

Not sure if my previous emails reached you — this is my last one on this thread, I promise.

I wanted to share what happened when we sent a preview to a [same vertical] in [nearby city]. They went from "I don't need a website" to a full site with booking in about a week.

Here's what they said: "[short testimonial]"

You can see their live page here: [similar-business-preview-link]

Your preview is still live here: [preview-link] — claim it any time.

Either way, good luck with [Business Name]. You've got something good going there.

—
[Your Name]
[unsubscribe link]

---

### Final Email (Day 21 — Soft Urgency)

**Subject:** Last one from me 👋

---

Hi [First Name],

I'll leave you alone after this, but wanted to let you know: we're cleaning up unclaimed previews at the end of the month.

Your preview for [Business Name] will stay live for another [X] days unless you claim it.

Claim it here: [preview-link] (takes 60 seconds)
Remove it here: [remove-link]

No hard feelings either way.

—
[Your Name]
[unsubscribe link]

---

## 9. COLD DM TEMPLATE (Instagram/Facebook)

### Version A — Casual / Visual Business

```
Hey [First Name]! 👋

We came across [Business Name] and built a free preview of what your website could look like.

It's got your photos, your hours, and a contact form — all on one page that actually looks good on phones.

Here's the link → [preview-link]

No catch, it's just our way of showing what we do. Would love to hear what you think! 🙌
```

### Version B — Service Business / Direct

```
Hey [First Name] — quick question.

When someone Googles "[service] in [city]", what's the first thing they see?

If it's not you, this might be worth a look → [preview-link]

We auto-built a page for [Business Name]. It's free to claim, free to edit. Zero commitment.

LMK if you have questions!
```

### Version C — Instagram Story Reply (if they post about needing a website)

```
Saw your story! We actually just built a preview for businesses like yours — it's free, takes 2 min to claim, and you can see exactly what it'd look like here: [preview-link]

Happy to walk you through it if useful.
```

---

## 10. LANDING PAGE COPY

### URL: [yourbrand].com/preview (or /free-website)

---

**Hero Section**

> ## We Built a Free Website for Your Business
>
> ### It only takes 60 seconds to claim, edit, or remove it.
>
> [See an example →] | [Claim your free preview →]

---

**The Hook**

> You probably don't need a fancy 10-page website.
> But you do need *something* — a page that shows up on Google, loads fast, and makes you look professional.
> We built one. For free. For businesses like yours.

---

**How It Works**

> **Step 1:** We find your business info online
> Name, hours, location, photos — we pull it from publicly available sources.
>
> **Step 2:** We build a preview — automatically
> In minutes, you get a beautiful one-page site.
>
> **Step 3:** Claim it, edit it, or let us build the full thing
> No pressure. No credit card. Just a better first impression.

---

**Examples**

> ### See It in Action
>
> [Business Type 1 — Preview Name →]
> [Business Type 2 — Preview Name →]
> [Business Type 3 — Preview Name →]

---

**FAQ**

> **Is this really free?**
> Yes. The preview is free. You can claim it, edit it, and use it at no cost. Upgrading to a custom domain and premium features is optional.
>
> **How did you get my business info?**
> We use publicly available data — your Google Business Profile, public social media, and business directories. Nothing private.
>
> **I already have a website.**
> Great! Is it mobile-friendly, fast, and something you can edit yourself? If not, we'd love to show you the comparison.
>
> **Can I remove my preview?**
> Always. One click and it's gone. No questions asked.
>
> **What happens to my data?**
> Your preview is governed by our [Privacy Policy]. You own your data and can request deletion at any time.

---

**CTA Section**

> ## Ready to See What Your Business Looks Like Online?
>
> ### [Claim Your Free Preview →]
>
> Already have a code? [Enter it here]

---

**Footer**

> [Your Company] © [Year]
> [Privacy Policy] | [Terms] | [Manage My Preview / Opt-Out]

---

## 11. KEY METRICS TO TRACK

| Stage | Metric | Target |
|---|---|---|
| Outreach | Emails/DMs sent | Track by vertical |
| Response | Open rate | >35% |
| Response | Reply rate | >8% |
| Preview | Click-through from outreach | >15% |
| Claim | Preview → Claimed | >5% |
| Upgrade | Claimed → Paid tier | >10% |
| Retention | Monthly churn (paid) | <8% |
| NPS | Customer satisfaction | >40 |

---

## 12. SALES MOTION SUMMARY

```
COLD PROSPECT
    │
    ▼
Email #1 — Preview Reveal (value-first, no pitch)
    │
    ▼
[Views preview] ──── No ────→ Follow-up sequence
    │
   Yes
    │
    ▼
Claim Page (email capture)
    │
    ▼
Dashboard — see preview, see upgrade CTA
    │
    ├── Upgrade to paid tier → WIN
    ├── Free forever (passive) → Nurture + referral ask
    └── Opt-out → Remove immediately, suppress from outreach
```
